title: Spying for Pakistan: Punjab man held for using highway CCTV to track Army movement
content: A Pathankot resident, Baljit Singh, has been arrested for allegedly spying for Pakistan-based handlers. He reportedly installed an internet-based CCTV camera near a bridge on NH-44 to monitor Army and paramilitary movements, transmitting live feeds to his handlers. Singh confessed to receiving directions from Dubai and being paid Rs 40,000.

title: 42 MLAs skip oath in God’s name during Kerala assembly swearing-in ceremony
content: Kerala's 16th Legislative Assembly's swearing-in ceremony showcased diversity as MLAs chose different languages and opted for solemn affirmations or oaths in God's name. Political rivals displayed camaraderie, and CM V.D. Satheesan reiterated his full name, addressing recent caste-related criticism. A symbolic gesture of reconciliation occurred between Pro-tem Speaker G. Sudhakaran and Pinarayi Vijayan.

title: 'Quran mandates Qurbani': AJUP chief Humayun Kabir stirs row after Bengal cattle slaughter curb
content: A political storm erupted in West Bengal as AJUP chief Humayun Kabir vowed to conduct cow slaughter for Eid, defying state restrictions. He insisted the Quran mandates it, challenging the government's notice. BJP countered, emphasizing law enforcement and opposing illegal slaughter, while some Muslim leaders urged respect for cows.

title: ‘If 60 wickets aren’t enough, scrap Ranji’: Vengsarkar slams selectors over Nabi snub
content: Cricket legend Dilip Vengsarkar is absolutely fuming! He's slammed the selectors for completely snubbing Auqib Nabi, the Ranji Trophy's top bowler, for the Afghanistan Test.  Despite Nabi's incredible 60 wickets and being named Player of the Tournament for leading J&K to their first title, they apparently passed him over due to 'pace concerns'.

title: Tamil Nadu cabinet expansion: CM Vijay inducts VCK, IUML MLAs as ministers
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin expanded his cabinet, inducting Vanni Arasu from VCK and AM Shahjahan from IUML. This move strengthens coalition representation, bringing the total number of ministers to 33. The expansion follows a recent reshuffle, with key portfolios like Finance and Revenue reassigned.

title: CEO of one of France’s biggest engineering groups Bouygues issues 'America warning'
content: Europe faces a stark warning from Bouygues CEO Olivier Roussat regarding its over-reliance on US technology, particularly in AI and satellite connectivity. He urges the continent to develop its own independent infrastructure, citing risks in these critical future sectors. Roussat emphasized the need for European sovereignty, suggesting alternatives to services like Starlink are essential for technological self-reliance.

title: IMD issues red alert for 10 UP districts, warns of 'very severe' heatwave
content: Uttar Pradesh faces a severe heatwave as the IMD issues a red alert for 10 districts, warning of extreme temperatures.  An orange alert covers 34 districts, with a yellow alert for 17 more.  Temperatures are expected to rise further in eastern UP.  Authorities are on high alert, with health advisories and preparedness measures in place.

title: Dowry demands, beatings never stopped, claims family of Delhi woman who fell to death
content: A young woman died after allegedly falling from her in-laws' terrace, with her family claiming she was subjected to severe dowry harassment and physical abuse. Despite their efforts to appease demands, the abuse escalated, leading to her desperate final call for help before her death. Police have arrested her husband and his brother.

title: Mark Zuckerberg's email to employees has a 'goodbye note' for those laid off
content: Meta has initiated significant layoffs, sending emails to thousands of employees whose positions are being eliminated.  Company leadership confirmed the decision to reduce headcount for increased efficiency.  Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg also sent a company-wide memo expressing gratitude to departing employees and outlining future company direction.  He assured remaining staff that no further company-wide layoffs are expected this year.

title: Pat Cummins: 'You want to see Abhishek, Klaasen, Trav, Ishan at their full flight'
content: Pat Cummins opens up on SRH’s fearless brand of cricket, the changing landscape of T20 batting, workload management, and surviving as an all-format fast bowler. The Australia captain also reflects on leadership, adapting to modern cricket, and the “wow” moment Vaibhav Sooryavanshi produced while taking on bowlers in the IPL

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to group fighting ‘overpaid CEO tax’
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

title: US-Iran war: Will rupee hit 100 versus US dollar soon?
content: ​Rupee depreciation is not new, what is new however is the rapid pace at which it is falling. It’s the biggest risk for India’s external sector resilience at the moment. In just over a year, the rupee has depreciated over 14%.

title: Actor Hemant who worked with Aamir, Ranveer, Big B, more exposed as absconding murder convict
content: After a 12-year game of hide and seek, the notorious actor Hemant, also known as Nagindas Parshottamdas Modi Vaishnav, was finally captured by the Ahmedabad Crime Branch. The actor had been on the run since he absconded on parole in 2014 while serving a life sentence for a gruesome murder in 2005. Read on to know more in detail.

title: IPL Playoffs: With 4 matches to go, what RR, PBKS, KKR and DC need to grab final spot
content: The IPL 2026 playoff race intensifies with Gujarat Titans securing a dominant win, virtually confirming their top-two spot. Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Sunrisers Hyderabad vie for the other top-two position. Rajasthan Royals are frontrunners for the final playoff berth, but Punjab Kings, Kolkata Knight Riders, and Delhi Capitals are still in contention, making the final league matches crucial deciders.

title: 'I get killed': Trump says Iran war stopping him from attending son's wedding
content: Amid escalating Iran tensions, President Trump expressed uncertainty about attending his son Donald Trump Jr.'s wedding this weekend in the Bahamas. He joked about facing criticism from the "fake news" regardless of his attendance, citing security concerns and the difficult timing due to international developments.

title: ‘Occultist’ rapes sister-in-law on pretext of warding off ‘Aghori’ curse
content: A faith healer in Bhopal allegedly raped his sister-in-law, claiming to perform an exorcism to remove an occult curse. He manipulated the family into isolating the survivor, then drugged and assaulted her. The survivor reported the incident to the police after regaining consciousness.

title: NEET row: NTA tells parliamentary panel leak did not happen from its system
content: A parliamentary committee questioned the NTA chief regarding the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, with the director stating the leak did not originate from their system. The committee also reviewed NTA reforms and discussed the impact of AI on education, alongside challenges faced by Aligarh Muslim University and the National Commission for Minority Educational Institutions.

title: 'This is our last talk': Angry over being woken up early by wife, man shoots self in Hathras
content: A disturbing event unfolded in Hathras district, where a man tragically succumbed to despair and took his life with an unauthorized gun after a morning quarrel with his wife regarding his wake-up time. Just before isolating himself in a room, the 35-year-old expressed to his brother-in-law that their conversation might be the last.

title: Twisha death case: Mother-in-law may lose bail, consumer panel post
content: Mother-in-law Giribala Singh faces potential cancellation of her anticipatory bail in the Twisha Sharma dowry death case. Police issued a final notice for her statement, warning of court action if she remains uncooperative. The government is also reviewing her position as president of the Bhopal district consumer commission.

title: India proposes 10-year jail, up to Rs 5 lakh fine for doping traffickers
content: India is taking a bold stand against doping in sports by proposing strict penalties for those involved in the distribution and trafficking of banned substances. With proposed jail sentences reaching ten years and substantial fines, this legislation seeks to defend the integrity of athletics and bring down the criminal networks that undermine it.

title: Air France flight to US diverted due to Ebola travel curbs
content: In response to a severe Ebola outbreak, countries worldwide are tightening travel restrictions. The US has implemented mandatory screenings for incoming passengers at Washington Dulles from areas affected by the outbreak, while Uganda has halted transportation connections with the Democratic Republic of Congo.

title: 'Nandri 2': Shubman Gill continues social media banter after GT eliminate CSK
content: Shubman Gill's Gujarat Titans secured a commanding victory over Chennai Super Kings, eliminating them from IPL 2026 playoffs. Following their second dominant win, Gill posted a witty social media caption, continuing a trend of viral post-match messages. GT's strong batting and bowling performance ensured their top-two finish, while CSK's campaign concluded.

title: Setback for US? Iran destroys over $1 billion worth MQ-9 Reaper drones since war began
content: Iran has reportedly downed over two dozen US MQ-9 Reaper drones, costing nearly a billion dollars and impacting a significant portion of Washington's advanced unmanned fleet. These losses, occurring amid ongoing conflict, highlight the vulnerability of expensive drones in contested airspace. The Pentagon faces challenges replacing these crucial intelligence and strike assets.

title: IPL 2026 Orange Cap: Sudharsan, Gill leapfrog Sooryavanshi to occupy top two spots
content: Gujarat Titans openers Sai Sudharsan and Shubman Gill have surged to the top two spots in the IPL 2026 Orange Cap standings after impressive performances against Chennai Super Kings. Sudharsan reclaimed the lead with 638 runs, while Gill sits second with 616 runs, highlighting their crucial role in GT's strong league stage finish.

title: Asian stocks today: Markets rise on hopes of US-Iran breakthrough; oil worries persist
content: Asian markets rallied Friday, buoyed by hopes of US-Iran peace talks, though volatile oil prices and Strait of Hormuz concerns fueled inflation worries. Tokyo led gains as investor sentiment improved. Despite diplomatic optimism, rising crude prices prompted reassessment of global interest rate expectations, with the US Federal Reserve potentially hiking rates later this year.

title: Michael Keating passed away at 79; fans pay tribute to EastEnders star
content: Acclaimed British actor Michael Keating, celebrated for his roles as Reverend George Stevens in 'EastEnders' and Vila Restal in 'Blake's 7', has passed away at 79. His extensive career spanned over four decades, gracing numerous beloved British television programmes. Fans and colleagues are mourning the loss of the talented performer, remembering his significant contributions to television.

title: Top shares to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Bajaj Broking Research has chosen Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers (GRSE), NBCC as the top stocks to buy on May 22, 2026:

title: 'Karuppu' BO day 7: Suriya's fantasy actioner crosses Rs 189 crores worldwide
content: Suriya's 'Karuppu' continues its strong theatrical run, nearing the Rs 200 crore global milestone. The fantasy action entertainer garnered Rs 189.05 crore worldwide in its first week, with the Tamil version leading collections. Despite mixed reviews, the film's blend of mythology and courtroom drama has resonated with audiences, ensuring steady box office performance.

title: Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding hit by Blake Lively blackmail drama
content: Taylor Swift has been pulled into fresh online controversy after viral rumors linked her to Blake Lively’s ongoing legal dispute with Justin Baldoni. Unverified claims and alleged leaked texts fueled blackmail speculation across social media. The drama comes as fans continue discussing Swift’s future with Travis Kelce and possible wedding plans. While no court documents confirm the allegations, the internet frenzy has intensified attention around Swift’s friendships, public image, and highly watched relationship with the Kansas City Chiefs star.

title: Jimmy Butler’s net worth: Warriors Star’s wealth, deals & luxury life
content: In 2026, Jimmy Butler is acceding to the funds he has collected in the NBA, in endorsements and in good business sense. All through his basketball career, BigFace Coffee, luxury investment and worldwide endorsement, Golden State Warriors star has amassed a net worth of $80 million to $90 million. Since then, his expanding roster of businesses and star status have garnered significant attention from the NBA world.

title: Congress leader Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan elected Kerala Speaker
content: In the elections held to Speaker’s post, Thiruvanchoor won 101 votes, while A.C. Moideen of Opposition LDF and B.B. Gopakumar of BJP-led NDA won 35 and 3 votes respectively. Speaker formally announces Pinarayi Vijayan as Opposition Leader

title: What else does U.S. have in store for India’s foreign policy? asks Congress
content: Congress general secretary Jairam Ramesh put out a video of Mr. Rubio's statement in Miami about his visit to India to attend the Quad meeting and also that of the Venezuelan President's

title: VCK, IUML join Vijay’s Cabinet in Tamil Nadu
content: With VCK’s Vanni Arasu and IUML’s Shahjahan on board, Vijay’s Cabinet in Tamil Nadu now has 35 Ministers

title: Fake call centres duping U.S. citizens busted in Bengaluru, man from Delhi, another from U.P. arrested
content: Police said the call centre employees used fake identities resembling those of U.S. officials to gain the trust of victims

title: Constitution not a privilege of few cosmopolitans, belongs to every citizen: CJI Surya Kant
content: "The Constitution is not a privilege of a few cosmopolitans who can afford cost-bearing processes and engage the best of the brains to assert something which our Constitution never intended to offer," CJI said

title: The Hindu’s journalist Afshan Yasmeen wins award
content: The event also honoured Latha Jagannathan, Medical Director and Managing Trustee, BMST Bengaluru, with the Lifetime Achievement Honour for her contribution to the field of blood cancer and blood disorder care

title: Travel operators in Bengaluru fear loss of livelihood if work-from-home increases, urge Centre for protection
content: Karnataka State Travel Operators’ Association (KSTOA) stated that the employee transport industry, which supports lakhs of jobs across the country, continues to play a crucial role in servicing India’s IT, corporate and industrial sectors

title: Andhra photographer Rukhiya Mohammed on her award-winning photographs of Eastern Ghats
content: Rukhiya Mohammed shares her thoughts on winning the Nature inFocus Award 2025 for  documenting the changing landscapes of the Eastern Ghats

title: ED raids in case against Sona Pappu, ex-Kolkata Police Deputy Commissioner
content: The premises include those of Mohammad Ali alias Max Raju, Saurav Adhikari, nephew of Biswas, and a "close associate" of Biswas named Ruhil Amin Ali, who is a Kolkata Police Sub Inspector, and Biswas' house in Murshidabad.

title: Congress workers clash with police in Jaipur during stir against NEET-UG paper leak
content: Leader of the Opposition Tika Ram Jully said while the paper leak was first exposed in Rajasthan, the State government “deliberately delayed” registering even a single FIR.

title: CJI’s ‘cockroaches’ comment & clarification: Oral remarks and institutional limits
content: Chief Justice Surya Kant’s “cockroaches” remark from the bench on May 15, and the clarification that followed, have revived a question the Supreme Court has tried to settle twice; the Constitution does not regulate casual judicial speech; the standard the Court has set out asks the public to read bench remarks as distinct from the formal opinion, and asks judges to keep that distinction from collapsing

title: 22 Ministers aged below 50: Cabinet signals generational shift in Tamil Nadu politics
content: Data on TVK Ministers, as on May 21, show that the Cabinet of 51-year-old Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay has the youngest average age among the three governments examined, while the 2021 Cabinet of M.K. Stalin had the oldest

title: Study reveals steady increase in thunderstorm and lightning activities in Bengaluru
content: Because heat and humidity are foundational building blocks for storm development, the probability of more severe localised thunderstorms is expected to rise in the coming years, says the study

title: Election Commission of India announces Rajya Sabha polls for 24 seats on June 18
content: ECI said the elections for the Rajya Sabha seats will be held in 10 States where the incumbents are retiring on different dates beginning June 21 to July 19

title: Content creator Shelvin James on playing Parashu in the Basil Joseph-Tovino Thomas starrer ‘Athiradi’
content: Shelvin James on working with Basil Joseph in Arun Anirudhan’s Athiradi and his decade-long career in content creation

title: Russia’s Sechenov University assigns ‘Visiting Professor’ status to Basappa Salundi
content: The status of ‘Visiting Professor of Sechenov University’ is an honorary, lifelong title for recognised external specialists

title: Manipur DGP moved to Cabinet Secretariat
content: Rajiv Singh has been appointed Secretary (Security) in the Cabinet Secretariat. He had been appointed as Manipur’s police chief by relaxing service conditions after an ethnic violence erupted in the State on May 3, 2023.

title: Take forward reforms, PM tells Ministers
content: The meeting of full Council of Ministers was held in the backdrop of U.S.-Iran war and difficult economic situation that could further result from the event

title: States should be allowed to conduct CET for medical courses: Karnataka CM
content: The Prime Minister and the Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan should bear the responsibility for the NEET question paper leak, says Siddaramaiah

title: Historic shift: Vijay’s Cabinet marks Tamil Nadu’s best SC representation till date
content: This is the first time that seven people from the SC community have been included simultaneously in the Tamil Nadu Cabinet

title: IIM Shillong joins elite league of global business schools
content: The institute received the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business accreditation recently

title: Kerala motorists flock to Mahe for cheaper fuel, raising revenue concerns
content: Following the construction of Mahe bypass, the service road has witnessed a surge in petrol pumps and liquor outlets catering to travellers seeking cheaper fuel and alcohol

title: Red alert issued as heatwave sweeps Uttar Pradesh
content: The alert has been issued for Banda, Chitrakoot, Kaushambi, Prayagraj, Fatehpur, Pratapgarh, Mirzapur, Varanasi, Bhadohi and Jaunpur districts, where severe heatwave conditions are likely during the day

title: Twisha Sharma death: Final notice served to ex-judge Giribala Singh for recording statement
content: Twisha Sharma’s family lawyer Ankur Pandey said that a petition had been filed before the principal Bench of the Madhya Pradesh High Court in Jabalpur challenging the anticipatory bail granted to Giribala Singh.

title: DMK faces a challenge as allies set to join TVK government
content: While the Congress abandoned the DMK soon after polls and joined Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay’s Cabinet on Thursday, the IUML and VCK are set to join the Ministry on Friday, signalling the end of their relations with the DMK

title: Ladakh leaders to meet Home Ministry officials on May 22, Sonam Wangchuk part of delegation
content: This is the first such meeting of members of the Leh Apex Body (LAB) and the Kargil Democratic Alliance (KDA), the two bodies representing Leh and Kargil districts respectively, since February 4; the KDA and LAB have been demanding constitutional safeguards such as Statehood and inclusion of Ladakh under the Sixth Schedule

title: PM’s five-nation tour delivered concrete outcomes, says Goyal; slams Rahul Gandhi’s ‘traitor’ remark
content: Commerce Minister was responding to LoP Rahul Gandhi’s remarks criticising the PM’s trip and calling him a “traitor”; Goyal accuses Congress of “treachery” for allegedly supporting infiltrators and terrorists

title: Lakshmi could have been a child bride
content: Teenage girls across five villages in Uttar Pradesh’s Chandauli district are resisting child marriage, supported by anganwadi workers who don’t want to see their own lives repeated in another generation. Ashna Butani meets the women leading and demanding change, in a State that sees the highest number of child brides in India

title: Sedition trials can go on if accused is willing: Supreme Court
content: In May 2022, the top court put on hold trials in sedition cases until the government completed its promised exercise “to re-examine and re-consider” the colonial-era provision of Section 124A

title: Mahila Congress seeks fresh probe into Hathras case, cites revelations in new documentary
content: All India Mahila Congress president Alka Lamba claims the documentary raised ‘serious and disturbing questions’ over the conduct of the police, Hathras district administration and the Uttar Pradesh govt. during the investigation

title: T.N.’s maternal healthcare indicators have improved in post-COVID-19 period, finds IIT-M study
content: The researchers say these improvements follow sustained investments in ambulance services, referral systems, and maternal healthcare programmes during and after the pandemic

title: Jharkhand family buries wrong body after mistaken identity; missing man returns alive
content: Police have now begun the process of exhuming the body to conduct a DNA test to identify the deceased correctly

title: Kerala Cabinet 2026: Full list of Ministers
content: The detailed portfolios will be submitted to the Governor and will be published via gazette notification

title: Karnataka on BJP high command’s radar as Nitin Nabin set for maiden visit to State
content: Recent developments, including the BJP’s landmark victory in West Bengal and the Opposition’s continued political grip on southern region, appear to have made the party national leadership to work towards re-establishing its hold in Karnataka

title: Swearing-in ceremony in Kerala Assembly presents interesting moments as T.K. Govindan and V. Kunhikrishnan take oath
content: T.K. Govindan, who had left the CPI(M) owing to internal disagreements, surprised everyone when he strode up to Pinarayi Vijayan first before taking the oath. Pinarayi half-rose to shake Govindan’s hand

title: Dress Code to be made mandatory in all State colleges, universities, says U.P. Government
content: The Minister added the government’s objective is not limited to improving academic quality alone, but also to creating a positive, disciplined, and value-based atmosphere in educational institutions.

title: KMRL offers measures to ease traffic snarls as monsoon nears
content: Additional traffic wardens will be deployed and safety measures will be stepped up along the Kakkanad route as part of monsoon preparedness amid ongoing Phase II work

title: Was Thirupparankundram temple closure delayed to facilitate Minister Nirmalkumar’s visit? asks Madras High Court
content: Justices G.R. Swaminathan and V. Lakshminarayanan call for a report from the Hindu Religious and Charitable Endowments department by May 29, 2026

title: Satheesan to open State-level Pravesanotsavam on June 1
content: Government Model Girls Higher Secondary School, Pattom, to host the celebration; Minister for General Education N. Samsudheen will preside

title: Shrinking shared spaces in a growing cityscape
content: Hyderabad is building upward at breakneck speed. But amid gated communities, flyovers and glass towers, the city’s shared public life is quietly shrinking. As open spaces retreat behind private walls, residents are increasingly searching for places to meet, play and simply exist without barriers, reports Lavpreet Kaur

title: Calcutta HC orders setting up SIT of CBI to probe hushing up charges in R G Kar rape-murder case
content: The Bench said that, considering the social ramifications of the gruesome incident, a three-member SIT would look into charges of evidence destruction and allegations made by the medic's parents that there were attempts to hush up and downplay the incident.

title: West Asia crisis hurting Indian tea exporters
content: According to the Indian Tea Association, about 46% of the country’s tea exports are concentrated in Iran, the UAE, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Turkiye, and Egypt

title: Gujarat Police trace 701 missing persons under ‘Operation Milap’ in two weeks
content: Additional Director General of Police (CID Crime and Railways) Ajay Choudhary said the operation is based on technical and human intelligence inputs and involves review of old case records and fresh field-level verification

title: Voter confusion fears over simultaneous polls ‘misplaced’, says ONOE JPC chairman
content: P.P. Chaudhary said the critical thinking ability of voters, especially in rural areas, should not be underestimated

title: From rallies to reels: political catchphrases turn dance anthems in Kolkata
content: Why are Kolkata club-goers dancing to Mamata Banerjee’s ‘Hamba Hamba’ and the BJP’s ‘Mach Chor’ ? From campaign songs and election slogans to viral reels and packed dancefloors, Bengal’s political techno remixes are blurring the lines between propaganda, memes and nightlife

title: Supreme Court declines to interfere with forest clearances for Adani’s Madhya Pradesh coal block project
content: The petitioner argued that a technical objection on the ground of delay ought not to preclude the court from examining the validity of the clearances

title: Himachal urban poll results reflect public confidence in Congress; party united with no groupism, says CM Sukhu
content: Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu said the Congress party has fulfilled all the promises made during the 2022 Assembly elections and has gone beyond its guarantees in delivering its commitments

title: Bangladesh-China talks underway for Tarique Rahman’s first foreign visit
content: Bangladesh may raise the need for infrastructure development, the relocation of Chinese industries to Bangladesh, the establishment of Chinese economic zones in Bangladesh, Prothom Alo reports

title: Bihar midday meal incident: HC asks whether NGO can continue providing meals
content: The court directed the Saharsa Superintendent of Police, the Food Safety Officer and PM Poshan officials to file affidavits explaining the seizure, sampling and laboratory examination processes.

title: New govt. hospital, preventing waterlogging are among my priorities, says Katpadi MLA
content: M. Sudhakar of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) says he has been in constant touch with Vellore Corporation to address civic concerns and formulate a long-term plan for Katpadi constituency

title: Indian accuses Vietnam Airlines staff of mocking family over Business Class seats
content: new money and old money are people who possess generational wealth . new money is those who have recently acquired it . an Indian doctor recently took to X (formerly Twitter) to share a harrowing experience involving alleged mockery from Vietnam Airlines staff .

title: Ebola outbreak forces India to put off Africa summit
content: the 4th India-Africa summit that India was to host from May 28 to 31 was postponed . this is the second time that an outbreak has led to postponement of the summit . the last summit India hosted was originally scheduled for Dec 2014 but could take place only 10 months later .

title: Iran In Talks With Oman For Permanent Hormuz Toll System
content: Situated between Iran to its north and Oman to the south, the strait connects the Persian Gulf to the Indian Ocean and normally handles a fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas supplies, as well as other commodities such as aluminium and fertilisers.

content: Unlike bank accounts, mutual funds, or fixed deposits, crypto does not come with a relationship manager, a branch office, or a standard recovery process

title: "We Can Do Without A Lot Of Drama": EU On Climate Talks At UN In Turkey
content: European countries were deeply frustrated by last year's COP30 climate conference in Brazil, which produced a watered-down final text that lacked any explicit mention of a fossil-fuel transition.

title: What Is GAESA, A Military Conglomerate At Centre Of US-Cuba Tensions
content: GAESA stands for Grupo de Administracion Empresarial - or 'business administration group.' It is a sprawling conglomerate of military-run businesses, widely seen as the island's most profitable and efficient.

title: NEET paper leak row: CBI grills doctor, teacher, parlour owner
content: education consultancy operator Shubham Khairnar, suspended junior college teacher manisha Mandhare, beauty parlour owner Manisha Waghmare and a latur-based paediatrician moved centre stage . a growing list of suspected beneficiaries are chasing money trails and digital footprints . over 22 lakh students wrote NEET-UG, which was cancelled on may 12 following allegations of widespread malpractice .

title: Astronomers detected a massive magnetic ‘twist’ inside the Milky Way
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
